*Expert Auspex, Supernatural Awareness:  Attackers must win a Simple Test versus the Kindred; otherwise, the attack automatically fails.

*Expert Auspex, Detect Lie:  Requires a Mental Challenge versus the target; if the user of the power wins, the target must tell the truth--the whole truth, to the point of mindless repetition unless stopped--in response to anything the Auspex-using Kindred asks.  If the result of the challenge is a tie, the target must simply reply truthfully.  If the result is a loss for the user of the power, the target may lie as he likes, but the empowered Kindred automatically knows when a lie is told.  Obfuscate of any level below Expert are ineffective against this power.

*Superior Auspex, Clairvoyance:  With this power, a vampire may "look in" on a place that he does not physically occupy, as with Psychic Projection.  However, the Kindred does not have to leave her body, and there is no way to detect or attack the "spy."

*Superior Auspex, Soul Scan:  A Kindred may locate anyone in the world she wishes to find.  It requires the expenditure of one Mental Trait, nothing more.

*Expert Celerity, Blur:  Spend 1 Blood Trait to gain a fifth action in a combat round; use of this level of Celerity cuts travel time to 1/16.

*Superior Celerity, Mercurial:  Spend 1 Blood Trait to gain a sixth action in a combat round; use of this level of Celerity cuts travel time to 1/32.

*Expert Presence, Love:  Win a Social Challenge versus a target; power duplicates the effect of a Blood Bond for the remainder of the game session.

*Superior Presence, Create Empathy:  May create bonds of emotion between two or more targets.  Requires a Social Challenge if one or neither target is willing.

Haven: Red Mountain

*Special Gangrel Combat Maneuver:  For every wound level of aggravated damage inflicted with Wolf Claws, victim loses 1 Physical Trait (which cannot be regained that session) and 1 Blood Trait.

*Expert Animalism, Awaken the Beast:  Spend 1 Social Trait to cause an opponent to frenzy.  There is no challenge involved; the person affected can spend a Willpower Trait to cancel the frenzy as normal.  Note that if a person has had Song of Serenity used successfully on them prior to the use of Awaken the Beast, the Song is negated.  Using Song of Serenity on the person again will, however, bring them out of frenzy.  This power can be used on oneself to negate Song, as well as cause frenzy as per the Merit:  Berserker.

*Expert Fortitude, Ignore Wound:  Spend 1 Willpower Trait to negate the effects of any one combat challenge.

*Expert Potence, Virility:  Win all ties regardless of who has more Traits; this also overrides Puissance.  Use of this force inflicts three base damage and is one of the more obvious breaches of the Masquerade.

*Expert Protean, Earth Control:  Activating this power is exactly as the Basic level of Earth Meld; however, this level allows the user to "swim" through the earth, moving underground at any speed desired.

*Expert Animalism + Protean, Horde of Rats:  User may spend one Blood Trait to cause her body to break down instantly into a mass of rats (two rats per pound of normal body weight).  The rats have a "group mind;" otherwise, they are ordinary--though vampiric--rats.  The rodents may immediately scatter or perform whatever other actions dictated by the vampire.  It is virtually impossible to destroy every rat, and the vampire may reform herself normally, with no penalties or damage, from even one rat.

Name: Anna Cooper.
Abandoned as a child at an orphanage in Dublin, Anna's singing talent was discovered and encouraged in a purely religious direction. Before taking the vows as a nun herself, she was embrased by Serenity, a Daughter of Cacophony. A year afterward, her sire was apprehended by Men of God and condemned as a witch. Afraid and alone, Anna committed herself to torpor. When she awoke in 1979, she assumed the name Calandra Terpsichore, taking her last name from the mythical Mother of the Sirens. In addition, Calandra decided that she would find one such as herself, with talent and no direction, to train. She left Ireland to pursue her search in America, spending over two decades in New York, examining the venues of struggling artists to find nothing but disappointment and badly trained voices. The time was profitable, however, for Calandra discovered new types of music, expanding her interest to opera, pop, punk and her pet genre, the ambient gothic strains that were beginning to become popular. In a fit of restlessness in 2000, Calandra traveled into the West, developing a love for singing in small goth/industrial clubs all around Colorado and California, and ending in Phoenix merely by chance as her traveling arrangements were rudely and perilously interrupted, forcing her to find day lodging in the city. Sick of traveling and curious about the new and warmer environment, Calandra settled as much as possible, determined to make a place for herself and to find the student she desired.

Around during the Dark Ages, Gideon was a successful thief and information broker.  He comitted several daring raids and was never caught by Kindred or mortal agents.  He came upon a promising Toreador by the name of Morgan Gallows who was trying to steal the Mona Lisa from the Louvre'.  Gideon decided to "help" him and they both wound up getting caught by the Camarilla Sheriff.  Thanks to Morgan's silver tongue, they escaped without facing death.  The two became fast friends and inseperable.  

Over the years they had made several enemies who wanted to see them in torpor or put to Final Death.  Not wanting to die, they struck a bargain with Madame Ghuil, to take over the Prince and  Scourge positions in the Raleigh/Durham area of North Carolina, and when the city is stable, she will appoint them as her Archons.  Refuse and we both face certain death.  

Not being stupid, they chose the former.  They now serve the Justicar as her Archons.

*Advanced Ritual, Ball of Abysmal Flame:  This Ritual requires the caster have Advanced Lure of Flames before attempting to learn or use it.  The material component of the ritual is a brass ring, which must be worn on the middle finger of the caster's right hand; only the person who casts this ritual may invoke it.  The ritual must be cast on the brass ring, which may only hold one casting at a given time.  By expending one Willpower Trait and engaging a target in a Mental Challenge versus their Physical Traits, the caster may hurl a blast of flame at a target that causes three aggravated wounds from fire damage; the victim must make a Simple Test to extinguish the flame.  If unsuccessful, the victim takes one additional aggravated wound per turn that the flame continues to burn.

*Expert Animalism, Species Speech:  By making contact with the target animal and expending one Social Trait, a Kindred may speak mentally with that animal.  The expenditure allows the power to last all session, but the Kindred must be in physical contact with the animal in order to speak with it.

*Expert Chimerstry, Far Fatuus:  The illusionist may spend 1 Mental Trait to sustain or project another Chimerstry effect to a location which they are not physically present at.  The illusionist must, however, have visited that location previously.

*Expert Chimerstry, Fatuus Mastery:  Lower levels of Chimerstry (Simple Illusion, Complex Illusion, Horrid Reality, Far Fatuus, Mass Reality) no longer require the expenditure of Traits or Willpower.

*Expert Fortitude, Ignore Wound:  Spend 1 Willpower Trait to negate the effects of any one combat challenge.

*Expert Protean, Earth Control:  Activating this power is exactly as the Basic level of Earth Meld; however, this level allows the user to "swim" through the earth, moving underground at any speed desired.

Cristof was once a Rom of the Kalderash family.  Born in 1797, he spent the first twenty-five years of his life in typical Gypsy fashion, attaining the position of baro over his kumpanyi at the age of twenty-three.  He was married to Ilina, a woman of the Phuri Dae family; it seemed that his life was going perfectly.
	Then, while camping one night outside a small town in northern Italy, his world fell apart.  The local law officials deemed the caravan a danger to public safety and attacked.  Ilina was one of the first struck down; a scream of unholy pain rose to the sky as Cristof hurled himself at his people's attackers.  Even as his hands closed on the neck of the politzia's leader, a lucky slash from the man's knife went through the baro's throat.
	But then his people did not fight alone--a woman of surpassing beauty moved like a whirlwind among them, bringing down the Italians with a savagery like a panther.  The last thing Cristof saw before his eyes darkened was her pale, splendid face, bending low above him.  He awoke a Ravnos, childe of Anyeta Inglescu; his wife, too, had survived, her wounds not mortal and thus not needing the Embrace's benefits to survive--instead, she was fed the powerful blood of the elder and became a ghoul.  After several years, with her full consent, Cristof Embraced his wife; the two are mutually and willingly Bound to each other, unusual for the independent Ravnos clan.
	Cristof devoted himself to the service of the Ravnos elder, he and his wife going so far as to take her last name in honor; when she crossed to America, he followed.  In Arizona, he is her second-in-command over the Ravnos, mortal and Kindred.

Way of Fire: ( Note: Seen as Psyic Development for this character untill noted otherwise )

Basic

Shatter: The Koldun spends a will power trait and a number of social traits, and points at an object.  The object will superheat and combust, either bursting into flame or shattering instantly.  The size of the object determines the number of social traits that must be expended by the Koldun: one trait for a small object (ie. A book), two for a medium sized object (a chair), and three traits for a large objects (a motorcycle).  Objects up to the size of a car cannot be affected by this power.  Living beings (and vampires) cannot be affected by this power. But can inflict up to one level of Aggravated damage as a by product of this power being used

Rouse the Molten Rock: The Koldun summons a small amount of molten lava to bubble up from the earth's core.  The Koldun spends a will power trait, and one social trait per round that the magma remains superheated. While heated, the magma does 3 levels of Aggravated damage per round to that which it comes in contact with.  The magma begins to cool once the Koldun quits fueling it with social traits, losing a level of Aggravated damage each round.  Only a small amount of magma appears (about enough to fill a bathtub), and it tends to flow downhill.

Intermediate

Gates of Magma: The Koldun spends a willpower trait and a social trait to call up a ring of magma ten feet tall that surrounds the Kodlun. The ring of magma can be maintained for two rounds per social trait spent. Any vampires attempting to approach the wall of magma must succeed in a courage challenge and suffer a level of Aggravated damage.  To cross through the magma requires that a vampire spend a willpower trait and suffer three levels of Aggravated damage. Normal mortals need not make the courage check, but do need to spend a will power trait if they intend to pass through the magma.

Heat Wave:

The Koldun summons a gust of superheated air that blasts her opponent.  She spends a willpower trait and makes a social challenge against her target, who resists with a physical challenge.  If the Koldun succeeds, the target takes 3 levels of lethal damage from the superheated air.  Kindred also lose three blood traits as the vitae evaporates from their system.
Note - This power has been modified from its original format in Blood Magic, and has been placed on a level of power on par with the Tremere Thaumaturgical power of Cauldron of blood.

**********************************************************************************************

The Way of Fire

The Tzimisce Koldun were masters of the elements, and this in no way was more visible than in their control over the elements. The Way of Fire was the least known of the Koldunic paths, due in large part because it was the least accessible of the elements.  It is even debated where the Tzimisce first learned this path. Some claim that they were taught the path by members of the Order of Hermes, perhaps House Flambeau. Others think that maybe there was a forgotten breed of the Tzimisce that lived in a more volatile climate. Certainly there is proof of a family of the Fiends living in India, caught up among the Hindu population.  Perhaps these fire dancers first discovered how to harness such terrible forces.  This is one of the most powerful and destructive of the sorcerous paths.  Storytellers are advised to keep careful wraps on this one.  The path given here is based upon that presented in Blood Magic: Secrets of Thaumaturgy, but like the Way of Spirit has been modified to fit in a live-action setting.  The Ways of Fire require a social challenge to be made against a target, who may bid physical traits if possible to dodge the effects.  These arts, like the rest of Koldun, require the expenditure of a willpower trait.  When employing the Way of Fire, the eyes of the Koldun turn a brilliant orange.  Note that the nature of the art protects the Koldun from her own arts, but not the arts of other Koldun.

Koldunism:

 Koldun is an unusual discipline.  It functions similar to Thaumaturgy, and the Tzimisce have developed the creative capacity to expand it beyond its basic paths.  Although there are only five paths mentioned in the book Blood Magic, it should here be noted that both Libellus Sanguinus 1 and Children of the Night state that there is indeed a Way of Blood.  Since it is not listed in Blood Magic, it can therefore be assumed to be identical to the already printed Path of Blood under Thaumaturgy.  It is quite likely that the Tremere "stole" the path originally from the Tzimisce, fine-tuning it in the process.  Moreover there are any number of possible minor paths, and the Way of Sorrow is an example of this presented in Clanbook: Tzimisce.  Other paths may so be developed. There are a number of differences between Thaumaturgy and Koldun*  There is no required primary path of Koldunism, although most Koldun learn the Way of Spirit first.*  Koldun paths always require the expenditure of a willpower trait to activate, never a blood trait.
*  The Way of Water and the Way of Air require mental traits and Challenges.  The Way of Fire and the Way of Spirit require social traits and challenges.  The Way of Earth requires physical traits and challenges; it may be assumed that the Way of Blood is also physical in nature.*  Koldunism affects the eye color of the wielder: Red for blood, aquamarine for water, orange for fire, brown for earth, pale blue for air, and white for spirit.  In addition, other effects (such as skin turning to rock for Way of Earth) may be evident.*  The retest for Koldun is the ability Koldunism.  No Koldunic sorcerer may have any path higher than the number of traits she Possesses in Koldunism.
